attorney.headline: Meet Attorney Pinho
attorney.name: Izi Pinho, Esq.
attorney.title: Founder & Managing Attorney
attorney.bio: Izi Pinho founded Pinho Law with a clear mission: to provide accessible, high-quality legal counsel to individuals, families and businesses navigating the U.S. immigration system and building businesses in Florida. With an academic background marked by excellence — graduating magna cum laude in Law from Stetson University College of Law and summa cum laude in Economics and Business Administration from Rollins College — Dra. Izi built her career with the same rigor she brings to every case. During her legal training, she published an article in the Stetson Law Review, served as its Editor, and received the Trust Award for the highest grade in Trusts and Estates. With deep roots in the Brazilian-American community and fluency in three languages, she brings both professional expertise and human understanding to every situation.
attorney.cta: Learn More About Dra. Izi Pinho

multilingual.headline: We Speak Your Language
multilingual.description: Every consultation, every document, every conversation — available in English, Portuguese, and Spanish. Because understanding your rights should never depend on translation.

items.question: How much does a consultation cost?
items.answer: The initial consultation has a fixed fee communicated at the time of booking. During the call, Dra. Izi Pinho evaluates your case, explains your legal options and presents a clear strategic plan — with no commitment to hire after the consultation.

items.question: How long does the immigration process take?
items.answer: It depends on the case type. The EB-2 NIW for Brazilians is CURRENT in 2026, with approvals ranging from 8 to 18 months. A marriage-based Green Card with a U.S. citizen averages 10 to 13 months. An E-2 visa can be approved in 3 to 6 months. We provide realistic timelines specific to your situation during the consultation.

items.question: Do you handle business law for immigrants?
items.answer: Yes — and that is one of our greatest differentiators. We assist immigrant entrepreneurs and executives with company formation, contracts, corporate structuring and Brazil-U.S. taxation, integrating immigration and business law under one roof.

items.question: What documents should I bring to my first meeting?
items.answer: Bring any relevant immigration documents (passport, current visa, USCIS notices), identification and any paperwork related to your case. After scheduling, we send a detailed preparation checklist so you arrive fully ready.

items.question: Can I open a company in the U.S. without a residency visa?
items.answer: Yes. Foreign non-residents can open an LLC in the U.S. without a work visa or Green Card. The process is 100% remote. At Pinho Law, we handle everything: Articles of Organization, EIN from the IRS, Operating Agreement and bank account setup.

items.question: Can a Brazilian get an E-2 visa?
items.answer: Brazil is not a signatory to the E-2 treaty. However, Brazilians with dual citizenship — Italian, Portuguese, Spanish, among others — may qualify. If you hold dual citizenship, schedule an evaluation so we can assess your profile.

items.question: What is the difference between EB-2 NIW and EB-1A?
items.answer: Both are paths to a Green Card without needing an employer sponsor. The EB-2 NIW requires demonstrating that your work is in the national interest of the U.S. The EB-1A requires proving extraordinary ability with recognized standing in your field — awards, publications, notable leadership. The best path depends on your profile and we assess this during the consultation.

items.question: Is my Brazilian will valid for assets I own in the U.S.?
items.answer: Not necessarily. In the U.S., real property is governed by the law of the state where it is located (lex situs). A will made in Brazil may not be recognized or may require a homologation process. A Revocable Living Trust is the most recommended structure to ensure your U.S. assets are transferred according to your wishes, without going through probate.

immigrationPage.headline: Immigration Law Services
immigrationPage.description: We guide individuals and families through every step of the U.S. immigration journey — from family petitions and green cards to work visas and citizenship. With deep knowledge of USCIS processes and a personal commitment to every client, we turn complexity into clarity.

businessPage.headline: Business Law Services
businessPage.description: Whether you are launching your first LLC or navigating a complex contract dispute, we provide the legal foundation your business needs to thrive in Florida. Our unique strength: we understand the intersection of immigration and entrepreneurship like no one else.

aboutPage.headline: About Pinho Law
aboutPage.description: Pinho Law was founded with one belief: that quality legal representation should be accessible to everyone — regardless of language, background, or the complexity of their situation. Based in Orlando, we serve clients across Florida with the personal attention of a boutique firm and the expertise of seasoned counsel.
aboutPage.approachDescription: We combine deep legal expertise with a genuine, approachable style. Every case we accept receives individualized attention, a strategy tailored to your goals, and transparent communication at every stage. We do not just handle cases — we guide people through some of the most important decisions of their lives.
aboutPage.difference1: Trilingual service in English, Portuguese, and Spanish — so nothing gets lost in translation
aboutPage.difference2: Combined expertise in immigration and business law — especially valuable for immigrant entrepreneurs
aboutPage.difference3: Responsive communication and transparent processes — you always know where your case stands
aboutPage.difference4: Deep roots in Florida's Brazilian-American and Latin American communities

attorneyPage.extendedBio: After witnessing firsthand the challenges that immigrants and their families face navigating the U.S. legal system, Izi dedicated her career to bridging the gap between complex legal processes and the real people behind every case. She founded Pinho Law to create a firm where clients feel truly heard — where every question is answered, every concern is addressed, and every strategy is built around the individual.
attorneyPage.educationDescription: With a background rooted in the Brazilian-American experience and a deep understanding of the challenges immigrants face, Izi brings both professional excellence and personal empathy to every case she handles.
